slides.title: Named Routes 📛
slides.content:
# Named Routes

Instead of creating routes inline, you can define them in `MaterialApp` using a map of names to builders.

* Define `routes` parameter in `MaterialApp`.
* Use `Navigator.pushNamed(context, routeName)`.
* Great for deep linking and organizing navigation.

quizData.question: How do you navigate to a named route '/details'?
quizData.explanation: pushNamed is the correct method for named routes.

slides.title: Setting Up Named Routes
slides.content: Define routes in MaterialApp and navigate:

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Use `onGenerateRoute` for dynamic routes

If you need to pass arguments or handle unknown routes, use `onGenerateRoute` instead of a static routes map.
